reportingProfile.description: Provider-aware reporting profile for the C1 LocalAI worker-mode baseline cycle. The profile combines benchmark measurements, minimal observability snapshots, cluster validation evidence, deployment metadata, placement metadata and diagnosis context into one cycle-scoped reporting package.

baseline.purpose: Provider-backed baseline for LocalAI worker-mode characterization on a proxmox-k3s-generated K3s cluster.
selectionCriteria: Keep the provider-backed baseline intentionally close to the historical application-level baseline so that infrastructure effects are not mixed with model, workload, or placement changes.
Use two RPC workers to preserve the validated LocalAI worker-mode topology before introducing resource, node-count, or placement variation cycles.
Use the smallest validated model family member to minimize footprint during the first provider-backed end-to-end cycle.
Use the controlled low-load workload to validate the full provider-backed pipeline before executing broader sweeps.
Use the explicit PL_COLOCATED placement profile for the first provider-backed baseline; later cycles can switch placement profiles while keeping model, workload, and infrastructure fixed.
lockRules: Do not mutate B1 after it has been used for a frozen provider-backed cycle.
Do not reuse B0 artifacts as evidence for B1.
Do not interpret B1 and B0 results as directly interchangeable without accounting for their different infrastructure profiles.
Use B1 as the provider-backed reference only after the declared proxmox-k3s cluster has passed validation.
Collect minimal observability evidence for B1 using metrics-server, kubectl snapshots and Locust artifacts before diagnosis and reporting.
baseline.notes: B1 intentionally inherits the application-level shape of B0 while switching to the INFRA_C1_1CP_2W_8C16G provider-backed infrastructure profile. This keeps the first provider-backed cycle focused on validating the provisioning-to-benchmark path rather than changing multiple dimensions at once.
baseline.scenarioProfileName: Provider-backed baseline for LocalAI worker-mode characterization on a proxmox-k3s-generated K3s cluster.

payload.description: First provider-backed baseline cycle generated from an infrastructure profile through proxmox-k3s before running LocalAI worker-mode benchmark workloads.

scope.primaryQuestion: Can the benchmark pipeline execute end-to-end on a K3s cluster generated through the declared infrastructure profile and proxmox-k3s provider?

rules: Do not use C0/B0 artifacts as evidence for C1 provider-backed execution.
Do not overwrite results/experimental-cycles/C0 when executing provider-backed experiments.
C1 must declare INFRA_C1_1CP_2W_8C16G or a deliberate replacement infrastructure profile before execution.
C1 must be provisioned through proxmox-k3s before LocalAI workloads are executed.
C1 must produce its own provisioning, validation, benchmark, diagnosis, reporting, completion-gate, and freeze artifacts.
Treat B1 as distinct from B0 even when application-level parameters are intentionally inherited for comparability.
C1 must resolve LC_REUSE_RETAIN_CLUSTER unless an explicit lifecycle override is recorded before execution.
C1 must write a cluster lifecycle manifest before provider-backed provisioning commands are executed.
C1 must not delete the generated cluster automatically unless destroyClusterAfterCycle is explicitly overridden.
C1 must resolve PI_C1_PROVIDER_BACKED_BASELINE before provider-backed provisioning commands are executed.
C1 must write a provisioning integration manifest that records provider config resolution, command logs, kubeconfig verification, and stop conditions.
Application-level benchmark execution must not start if provider-backed provisioning or kubeconfig verification fails.
C1 must resolve CV_C1_PROVIDER_BACKED_BASELINE before application deployment.
C1 must write a cluster validation manifest after kubeconfig verification and before LocalAI deployment.
Application-level benchmark execution must not start when cluster validation does not reach the validated status.
C1 must resolve AD_C1_PROVIDER_BACKED_BASELINE after cluster validation and before LocalAI deployment.
C1 must write an application deployment manifest that records Kustomize targets, expected resources, rollout checks, and smoke validation results.
Benchmark execution must not start when LocalAI deployment or smoke validation fails.
C1 must resolve MO_C1_PROVIDER_BACKED_BASELINE after LocalAI deployment and before diagnosis/reporting.

C1 must collect minimal observability evidence using K3s, metrics-server, kubectl snapshots, Kubernetes events, pod placement, restart counts, pending pods, and Locust client-side artifacts when available.
Pending pods, failed pods, missing optional metrics and Kubernetes warning events must be recorded as evidence instead of being silently discarded.
C1 must resolve FR_C1_PROVIDER_BACKED_BASELINE before the cycle is frozen.
C1 freeze must snapshot provider, provisioning, validation, deployment, observability, benchmark, diagnosis, reporting and completion-gate evidence under results/experimental-cycles/C1/artifacts.
C1 freeze must not mutate or reuse C0/B0 historical artifacts as provider-backed evidence.
C1 freeze must verify completion-gate readiness before preserving final cycle artifacts.
A normal C1 freeze rerun must preserve an existing artifact snapshot unless a force option is explicitly provided.

nextCyclePreparation.recommendedNextPurpose: Resource variation over provider-backed infrastructure profiles after C1 has validated the end-to-end provider-backed baseline path.

infrastructureProfile.description: Infrastructure profile used by the first provider-backed baseline cycle generated through proxmox-k3s.

placementProfiles.profileSelectionPolicy: C1 uses PL_COLOCATED as the explicit provider-backed baseline placement. Later placement-focused cycles can switch only placementProfileId while keeping infrastructure, model, workload, worker count, and protocol fixed.

stateModel.profileStatus: Configuration-profile lifecycle state. This is the canonical meaning of the backward-compatible status field.
stateModel.configurationState: Whether the cycle configuration is active, locked, superseded, or deprecated.
stateModel.executionState: Latest known execution outcome for the cycle or campaign.
stateModel.status: Backward-compatible alias of profileStatus; execution outcomes must be read from executionState.

infrastructureSummarySemantics.description: The cycle uses one fixed infrastructure profile as the common context for all scenarios.

payload.profileName: Provider-Backed Baseline Historical-Size Two-Worker K3s Cluster
payload.purpose: Canonical provider-backed baseline profile that reproduces the historical two-worker resource envelope while using proxmox-k3s for cluster materialization.

lifecycle.policySelection: Cycles may override the profile default only when the override is declared in the cycle profile and captured in the lifecycle manifest.

nodeNaming.notes: Node names are compatibility handles; infrastructureProfileId remains the authoritative environment identifier.

provisioning.notes: Provider configuration is resolved through the proxmox-k3s provider binding associated with this infrastructure profile.

benchmarkCompatibility.notes: This is the default C1 provider-backed baseline profile because it matches the historical worker resource envelope more closely than the lower-resource 4C8G fallback.

comparisonPolicy.policy: Use infrastructureProfileId as the primary comparison key; identical node names do not imply equivalent resources or provisioning state.

observabilityAssumptions.notes: Minimal K3s observability is sufficient for this profile unless a later configuration explicitly requires additional add-ons.

placementCompatibility.notes: Placement compatibility is declared at the profile level so that topology comparisons remain traceable to the infrastructure profile that materializes the cluster.

governance.providerToolBoundary: proxmox-k3s creates and deletes K3s clusters; the benchmark suite resolves bindings and orchestrates workload execution.
governance.comparisonPolicy: Use infrastructureProfileId as the primary comparison key; identical node names do not imply equivalent resources or provisioning state.
governance.vmidCollisionPolicy: Use the declared VMID allocation policy and clean up partial provider-created VMs before retrying create operations with the same cluster_name.

lifecycleCollisionGuard.rationale: This binding intentionally uses the stable genai-pb naming policy. Collision evidence is recorded at provisioning time so reuse and ephemeral workflows remain explicit.

payload.purpose: Resolve the C1 infrastructure profile, lifecycle policy, and proxmox-k3s provider binding, then create or refresh the K3s cluster before application-level benchmark execution.

plan.description: Resolve cycle, infrastructure, provider binding, lifecycle, and provider configuration without executing provider commands.

provision.description: Create or converge the provider-backed cluster and verify the kubeconfig written by the provider create command.

kubeconfig.description: Refresh kubeconfig for an already-created provider-backed cluster.

destroy.description: Delete a provider-backed cluster only when explicitly confirmed and allowed by lifecycle policy.

governance.providerToolBoundary: This profile orchestrates provider execution from the benchmark suite but does not change proxmox-k3s or make it aware of LocalAI workloads.
governance.documentationPolicy: This profile is operational configuration and not project documentation.
governance.comparisonPolicy: Provisioning artifacts must be retained under the cycle-specific results tree so future comparisons can distinguish infrastructure differences from workload differences.

lifecycleCollisionGuard.rationale: The benchmark suite may reuse stable provider cluster names and IP allocations across infrastructure profiles. The guard records identity and collision evidence before provider execution without making proxmox-k3s workload-aware.

payload.purpose: Validate that the C1 provider-backed K3s cluster is reachable, structurally consistent with the declared infrastructure profile, and ready to receive LocalAI worker-mode workloads.

governance.providerToolBoundary: Cluster validation consumes the kubeconfig produced by the provider workflow but does not modify proxmox-k3s.
governance.documentationPolicy: This profile is operational configuration and not project documentation.
governance.comparisonPolicy: Cluster validation artifacts must be retained under the cycle-specific results tree before application deployment and benchmark execution.

downstreamConsumers.reason: Minimal observability consumes the validated kubeconfig, Metrics API and Kubernetes snapshots after the cluster validation gate passes.

payload.purpose: Deploy the LocalAI server and RPC worker topology for the C1 provider-backed baseline after the generated K3s cluster has passed validation.

placement.profileResearchQuestion: How does a compact LocalAI topology behave when communication distance is minimized but CPU and memory contention may increase?

governance.providerToolBoundary: Application deployment consumes the validated kubeconfig produced by the provider workflow but does not modify proxmox-k3s.
governance.documentationPolicy: This profile is operational configuration and not project documentation.
governance.comparisonPolicy: Application deployment artifacts must be retained under the cycle-specific results tree before benchmark execution.
governance.placementPolicy: Deployment uses an explicit placement profile so that future placement-only comparisons can change topology without changing model, workload, or infrastructure.
governance.minimalObservabilityPolicy: Minimal observability evidence is collected from K3s, metrics-server, kubectl snapshots and Locust artifacts.

payload.placementIntent: Place the LocalAI server and active RPC workers on the same worker node to minimize intra-application communication distance.
payload.researchQuestion: How does a compact LocalAI topology behave when communication distance is minimized but CPU and memory contention may increase?

selectorPolicy.rationale: Stable node names are retained for compatibility with the current Kustomize overlays; label-based node-pool selection is the preferred direction for future generated clusters.

serverPlacement.placementNotes: The current server manifest is still node-name compatible; later topology profiles can replace this with a label-based selector.

mappingSemantics.activeWorkerNodeMapByWorkerCount: Application-level worker-count mapping currently defined for W1, W2, W3 and W4, with all active RPC workers co-located on genai-pb-worker-02.

compositionSelectionPolicy.selectionRationale: This placement profile varies the LocalAI application topology by active RPC-worker count. Infrastructure worker-node count is not the primary selector for this profile.

expectedBenefits: lower communication distance between server and RPC workers
expectedRisks: higher CPU and memory contention on the selected node

governance.comparisonPolicy: Placement comparisons must keep infrastructure, model, workload, worker count, protocol, and metric collection fixed unless a cycle profile explicitly declares otherwise.

payload.purpose: Collect and normalize the minimum client-side and Kubernetes-side observability evidence required before benchmark diagnosis and reporting.

B1.label: B1 (Provider-backed baseline for LocalAI worker-mode characterization on a proxmox-k3s-generated K3s cluster.)

scenario.purpose: Provider-backed baseline for LocalAI worker-mode characterization on a proxmox-k3s-generated K3s cluster.
selectionCriteria: Keep the provider-backed baseline intentionally close to the historical application-level baseline so that infrastructure effects are not mixed with model, workload, or placement changes.
Use two RPC workers to preserve the validated LocalAI worker-mode topology before introducing resource, node-count, or placement variation cycles.
Use the smallest validated model family member to minimize footprint during the first provider-backed end-to-end cycle.
Use the controlled low-load workload to validate the full provider-backed pipeline before executing broader sweeps.
Use the explicit PL_COLOCATED placement profile for the first provider-backed baseline; later cycles can switch placement profiles while keeping model, workload, and infrastructure fixed.
lockRules: Do not mutate B1 after it has been used for a frozen provider-backed cycle.
Do not reuse B0 artifacts as evidence for B1.
Do not interpret B1 and B0 results as directly interchangeable without accounting for their different infrastructure profiles.
Use B1 as the provider-backed reference only after the declared proxmox-k3s cluster has passed validation.
Collect minimal observability evidence for B1 using metrics-server, kubectl snapshots and Locust artifacts before diagnosis and reporting.
scenario.notes: B1 intentionally inherits the application-level shape of B0 while switching to the INFRA_C1_1CP_2W_8C16G provider-backed infrastructure profile. This keeps the first provider-backed cycle focused on validating the provisioning-to-benchmark path rather than changing multiple dimensions at once.
scenario.scenarioProfileName: Provider-backed baseline for LocalAI worker-mode characterization on a proxmox-k3s-generated K3s cluster.
